title: Micro-resolution ultrasonic nondestructive imaging method
abstract: A system and methods of nondestructive testing are described. The system includes an immersion ultrasonic probe and a laser vibrometer. The immersion ultrasonic probe and a sample are immersed in a fluid contained in an immersion tank and the laser vibrometer is disposed outside of the immersion tank. A tightly focused ultrasonic beam from the immersion ultrasonic probe and a laser beam from the laser vibrometer are both transmitted upon a sample, the laser beam being transmitted through the wall of the immersion tank. Since the ultrasonic beam is tightly focused and the laser beam samples only a small area impinged by the ultrasonic beam, microscopic resolution is obtained.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A non-destructive testing (NDT) apparatus, comprising: an immersion ultrasonic probe, an immersion tank, a laser vibrometer, and a sample holder. 2. The NDT apparatus of claim 1, wherein the immersion ultrasonic probe comprises a piezoelectric transducer comprising a reflective mirrored finish. 3. The NDT apparatus of claim 2, wherein the reflective mirrored finish comprises gold. 4. The NDT apparatus of claim 1, wherein the immersion ultrasonic probe, and a portion of the sample holder are submerged within the immersion tank and wherein the laser vibrometer is disposed outside the immersion tank. 5. The NDT apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a 3-axis scanner operatively connected to the sample holder. 6. The NDT apparatus of claim 5, further comprising a processor configured to control the 3- axis scanner. 7. The NDT apparatus of claim 6, wherein the processor is configured to control the immersion ultrasonic probe. 8. The NDT apparatus of claim 6, wherein the processor is configured to control the laser vibrometer. 9. The NDT apparatus of claim 6, wherein the processor is configured to receive digital signals derived from the laser vibrometer. 10. The NDT apparatus of claim 1, wherein the immersion ultrasonic probe is configured to generate an ultrasonic signal having a frequency of 5 MHz to 30 MHz. 1 1. The NDT apparatus of claim 1, wherein the immersion ultrasonic probe is configured to generate an ultrasonic signal having a frequency at least 10 MHz. 12. The NDT apparatus of claim 1, wherein the immersion ultrasonic probe is configured to generate an ultrasonic signal having a beam focal diameter of less than 300 micrometers measured at -3 dB from center peak-amplitude. 13. The NDT apparatus of claim 1, wherein in the immersion ultrasonic probe comprises a body and wherein the laser vibrometer is configured to emit a beam of laser light that passes through the body before reaching a sample held by the sample holder. 14. The NDT apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a 3-axis scanner, wherein the apparatus is configured to receive inputted testing parameters and to control the 3-axis scanner based on the inputted testing parameters. 15. The NDT apparatus of claim 14, wherein the inputted testing parameters relate to resolution. 16. An immersion ultrasonic probe, comprising: a piezoelectric transducer comprising a reflective mirrored finish, and an ultrasonic lens. 17. The immersion ultrasonic probe of claim 16, wherein the reflective mirrored finish comprises gold. 18. An immersion ultrasonic probe, comprising: a piezoelectric transducer comprising an orifice, an ultrasonic lens comprising an orifice, 

 a body, and a tube, wherein the ultrasonic lens is disposed at a front side of the body and wherein the tube optically connects the orifice of the ultrasonic lens to the back of the immersion ultrasonic probe.
